pattern: Tracking each person's trust in-degree minus out-degree reveals the unique judge as the one trusted by all others while trusting nobody.
hints: The judge is trusted by everyone else but trusts no one, so think about how many people trust each person versus how many that person trusts.
Give each person a score that rises when someone trusts them and falls when they trust someone.
The judge's score must equal n minus one: trusted by all other n minus one people and trusting nobody; scan for exactly that score.

edge_inputs.description: two people, the trusted one is the judge
three people converge trust on one
candidate judge also trusts someone so no judge
single person with no trust is the judge
everyone trusts but no one is universally trusted
two people with no trust edges has no judge
four people all trust one who trusts none

stress.description: construct a guaranteed judge then add random non-judge edges
stress.note: judge never appears as a truster so it stays valid
